name: Coder Phase
description: The 3-Task/3-Step graph `darkmux mission launch coder-phase` runs for one phase: prepare a git worktree, dispatch a specialist role into it, run mechanical verify (code-reviewer). A strict linear chain (worktree -> coder -> verify, each depends_on the last). This document is the EXECUTABLE source of truth for that graph; the launch path resolves role/workdir/image as task_overrides and calls mission_config::interpret, and the Tier-3 step kinds it references (mission.worktree/mission.coder/mission.verify) live in src/coder_phase.rs, the launch-owned module (#1426 ship-4 retired the `mission run` verb and its hand-built default_phase_graph launcher). ID SCHEME (placeholder-prefix rule): every task/step id below carries the literal `build-` prefix standing in for the launcher's real composed phase id -- mission_config::interpret substitutes it at launch (`{phase_id}-worktree`, `{phase_id}-coder`, `{phase_id}-verify`; steps append `-step`), so persisted ids stay stable across launches, byte for byte.

inputs.name: workdir
inputs.description: The git worktree path (mission_run's wt_path) every task in this phase's dispatches run in. Set on Task.workdir at launch time -- this schema deliberately has no workdir field on TaskConfig (see mission_config.rs's TaskConfig doc): it is genuinely per-launch, not part of the static shape.

inputs.name: role
inputs.description: Which specialist role the coder task dispatches (default_phase_graph's `role` param). Overrides the coder task's role_id default of "coder" below, and is substituted into that task's description -- the Rust builder writes the description dynamically as `dispatch \`{role}\` into the worktree`.

inputs.name: image
inputs.description: Optional container image override for the coder task's dispatch (default_phase_graph's `image` param). Absent -> the runtime's default image.

inputs.name: base
inputs.description: Git base branch/ref the phase worktree branches from (mission_run's add_worktree `base` arg).

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: The worktree's new branch name (mission_run's add_worktree `branch` arg).

inputs.name: mission_id
inputs.description: The owning Mission's id -- flows into every step kind's own emit_step_result flow record (mission.worktree/mission.coder/mission.verify all carry it).

phases.description: prepare the phase worktree, dispatch a specialist role into it, run mechanical verify

notes: role_id here is the DEFAULT; the `role` input can override it at launch time (default_phase_graph's `role` parameter is caller-supplied, not fixed to "coder").

`coder` in the description is likewise the default role name -- the Rust builder writes the description dynamically (`dispatch \`{role}\` into the worktree`), so the launcher substitutes the resolved role at launch.
